ey

Get 6 Hecke bebey Meum Trina nto

‘College of Hducation, “loany, New York, Sept. 12, 1961

Inprovemen+ ef the +caching of English will bethe goal of the Institute in English spensere’ by the College Entrance b:amination Beard and the New York State Univers ity College of Eduaation, Albany, to be held a+ the college h the smmer of 1962, Three area professors who will b the instructors in +he Institute have returned from a three-week planning session at the “nive-sity of Michigan, a+ 4nn Arbor.

The Gommission on English of the College Entrance Exan- ination Besrd has chosen the College of Educati>n as one@ twenty colleges acress +he country +e be a center for English teachers +e B+udy and @velep new materials and methods of teachi-g. Forty-five teachers from public, private, and parochial scheols within fifty m‘les of Albany will be chosen +o p rticipate.

Graduate credit will be given for the Ins+itute courses which were planned by +he instructors, Dr, Gretchen “aulus, Asseciate Professor of English a+ the Uollese ¢ Bdweation, Dr. Carl Niemeyer, Yrefesser ¢ English and Chairman of the ona tea Department of Union Yollege, Schenectdady, and Mr, William C, Kraus, As istant

rrefecseref Educat’on (English) and supervisor a+ +he Milne School,

College of Education.
